data.title: Darden Restaurants reports mixed Q3 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Darden Restaurants press release (NYSE:DRI): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.80 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $3.16B (+6.4% Y/Y) misses by $60M.
  • Darden's Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.40 per share on the Company's outstanding common stock. The dividend is payable on May 1, 2025 to shareholders of record at the close of business on April 10, 2025.
    • Total sales of approximately $12.1 billion vs. $12.10B consensus (prior approximately $12.1 billion)
    • Same-restaurant sales growth of approximately 1.5%*
    • New restaurant openings of 50 to 55
    • Total capital spending of approximately $650 million
    • Total inflation of approximately 2.5%
    • An effective tax rate of approximately 12.5%
    • 118.3 million weighted average diluted shares outstanding
    • Adjusted diluted net earnings per share from continuing operations of $9.45 to $9.52, excluding approximately $47 million, pre-tax, of Chuy's transaction and integration related costs vs. $9.48 consensus (prior $9.40 to $9.60).

data.title: Gambling.com Non-GAAP EPS of $0.35 beats by $0.11, revenue of $35.31M beats by $0.17M
data.description:
• Gambling.com press release (NASDAQ:GAMB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.35 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $35.31M (+9% Y/Y) beats by $0.17M.
  • Gambling.com Group today reiterated the 2025 full-year revenue and Adjusted EBITDA guidance originally provided on February 19, 2025. The Company expects full year revenue of $170 million to $174 million and Adjusted EBITDA of $67 million to $69 million.

data.title: OptiNose climbs after take-private deal with Paratek Pharma
data.description:
Shares of OptiNose (NASDAQ:OPTN) climbed ~61% in the premarket on Thursday after the specialty pharmaceutical company agreed to be acquired by antibiotic drugmaker Paratek Pharmaceuticals in a take-private deal worth up to $330M.

Per the deal terms, privately held Paratek will acquire OptiNose’s (NASDAQ:OPTN) outstanding shares for $9 per share in cash in addition to a contingent value right worth $5 per share, subject to certain sales milestones achieved by OPTN’s lead asset Xhance.

The upfront consideration of $9 per share implies a 50% premium to Wednesday's closing share price of Yardley, Pennsylvania-based OptiNose (NASDAQ:OPTN).

After approving Xhance in 2017 to treat chronic rhinosinusitis (CRS) with nasal polyps, the FDA broadened the drug-device combination’s label last year to include CRS patients without nasal polyps, increasing its addressable market by about ten times.

The transaction, expected to close in mid-2025, is funded by entities including Novo Holdings, the investment arm of Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) controlling shareholder, which acquired Paratek Pharmaceuticals in 2023 with B-FLEXION.

data.title: European markets slip as German stocks weigh, focus on central bank verdicts
What Tesla and Elon Musk need to do to flip the script - Wedbush
data.description:
Wedbush Securities backed its Outperform rating on Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and $550 price target on Thursday, although the firm thinks Elon Musk needs to make a few moves to flip the narrative. 

Analyst Dan Ives thinks Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) and Elon Musk are facing a defining chapter in their future, and warned that how Musk handles this next few months will be pivotal to the long-term growth trajectory of the company.

Ives and his team think there are two main things that Musk must do in the next few months to stop the current brand crisis from getting worse and restore investor confidence.

"1) Formally announce Musk is going to balance DOGE and being Tesla CEO. Musk needs to make a statement and his actions speak louder than words. We would expect this to happen either before or during the 1Q earnings conference call in early May. Investors need to see Musk take a step back and balance his DOGE and Tesla CEO roles...if he does this the heat from Musk around DOGE will start to dissipate among most of the critics and this will leave a scar for Tesla,....but not permanent brand damage."

"2) Give the roadmap and timing for the new lower cost vehicles to be released into the market in 2025 along with the unsupervised FSD roll-out in Austin in June. With a Model Y refresh, inventory issues, and a host of demand issues with Musk brand damage a worry...there is one person Tesla investors need to hear from...Musk."

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) showed a 1.8% decline in premarket action to $231.58 vs. the 52-week range of $138.80 to $488.54. The EV stock is down about 42% on a year-to-date basis. Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha analysts both have a consensus Hold rating on Tesla (TSLA).

data.title: Caleres reports mixed Q4 results; initiates Q1 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Caleres press release (NYSE:CAL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.33 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $639.2M (-8.3% Y/Y) misses by $6.85M.
  • Returned $74.7 million to shareholders in fiscal 2024 through share repurchases and quarterly dividends;
  • Now expects 75% of our Brand Portfolio, and an even greater percentage of our Lead Brands’ sourcing to be outside of China by the second half of 2025
  • Expects fiscal 2025 consolidated net sales to be down 1% to up 1% compared to fiscal 2024;
  • Expects fiscal 2025 earnings per diluted share of $2.80 to $3.20;
  • Guidance excludes the previously announced acquisition of Stuart Weitzman, which is expected to close in the summer of 2025.

  • Fiscal 2025 Outlook

    Metric

    Net Sales

    Down 1% to Up 1% vs. estimated growth of 2.11% Y/Y

    Operating Margin

    5.1% to 5.6%

    Tax Rate

    ~23%

    GAAP EPS

    $2.80 to $3.20 vs. $3.40 consensus

    Capital Expenditures

    $50 to $55 million

    First Quarter 2025 Outlook

    Metric

    First Quarter Guidance

    Net Sales

    Down 5% to 6% vs. estimated growth of -1.01% Y/Y

    GAAP EPS

    $0.35 to $0.40 vs. $0.48 consensus

data.title: Lands' End Non-GAAP EPS of $0.57 in-line, revenue of $441.9M misses by $16.79M
data.description:
• Lands' End press release (NASDAQ:LE): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.57 in-line.
  • Revenue of $441.9M (-14.2% Y/Y) misses by $16.79M.

  • For the first quarter of fiscal 2025 the Company expects:

    • Net revenue to be between $260.0 million and $290.0 million.
    • Gross Merchandise Value expected to deliver flat-to-low single digits percentage growth.
    • Net loss to be between $9.0 million and $6.0 million and diluted loss per share to be between $0.29 and $0.19.
    • Adjusted net loss to be between $7.0 million and $4.0 million and Adjusted diluted loss per share to be between $0.22 and $0.13.
    • Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $9.0 million to $12.0 million.

  • For fiscal 2025 the Company expects:

    • Net revenue to be between $1.33 billion and $1.45 billion.
    • Gross Merchandise Value expected to deliver mid-to-high single digits percentage growth.
    • Net income to be between $8.0 million and $20.0 million and diluted earnings per share to be between $0.25 and $0.64.
    • Adjusted net income to be between $15.0 million and $27.0 million and Adjusted diluted earnings per share to be between $0.48 and $0.86.
    • Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $95.0 million to $107.0 million.
    • Capital expenditures of approximately $30.0 million.

data.title: M&A Snapshot: QXO to acquire Beacon Roofing Supply in $11B deal
Energizer Holdings strengthens its debt capital structure with extension of term loan and revolving credit facility
data.description:
Energizer Holdings (NYSE:ENR) announced on Thursday the successful extension of its $760 million term loan and $500 million revolving credit facility. The company said the transactions are leverage neutral and further strengthen its debt capital structure by extending maturities for both facilities at roughly the same interest rates, providing enhanced financial flexibility in the years to come.   

The new term loan matures in 2032, and bears interest at a rate equal to Secured Overnight Financing Rate plus 200 basis points per annum. The new revolving credit facility matures in 2030, and bears interest at a rate equal to Secured Overnight Finance Rate plus the applicable margin, based on leverage. The transactions extend the existing maturities of both facilities by more than four years, and the Company's weighted average maturity by over one year. 

CEO John Drabik said the latest refinancing further strengthens the company's debt capital structure and is a testament to the credit market's appreciation of Energizer's (EBR) track record of debt reduction and strong operating performance. "By extending the maturities on these facilities, we have continued to strengthen our balance sheet while maintaining a cost-efficient, flexible and prepayable debt capital structure. We will continue to evaluate opportunities to extend our debt maturities or improve our interest rate profile as we advance our debt paydown and deleveraging objectives," he highlighted. 

Shares of Energizer Holdings (NYSE:ENR) are down 13.3% on a year-to-date basis.
